title: cerinthe major subsp. gymnandra
intro: Cerinthe major subsp. gymnandra, also known as honey flower or blue sky flower, is a type of flowering plant in the borage family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of cerinthe major subsp. gymnandra are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, offering health benefits such as alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and soothing skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, making it a versatile ingredient in natural remedies.
